_comment: Cross-language contract for the recipe_id slug (ADR-024 #741). Both the Python compiler (scripts/tools/dx/custom_alerts/shape.py) and the Go exporter (components/threshold-exporter/app/pkg/config/custom_alert.go) MUST compute these exact slugs from these inputs — a drift silently breaks every on(tenant) group_left join. Deterministic, locale-free, map-order-independent. NOTE (TRK-326): `for` is part of the slug (control-plane static rule attribute) and is ALWAYS emitted as the `__for{for}` part (default 1m); it is the FINAL segment only for a no-selector recipe — a selector-bearing recipe appends the `__x{hash}` suffix AFTER it (see the #1008 note). NOTE (#1008 / F3): a recipe with >=1 selector carries a trailing `__x{16-hex}` = first-16-hex of SHA-256 over a length-prefixed canonical of the STRUCTURED shape identity (selectors are the only tenant-controlled free-form slug field; window/quantile/metric/for/group_by are charset/enum-bounded). A no-selector recipe stays byte-identical (no suffix). Add a case when adding a recipe/param.

vectors._note: #1008/F3: selectors present → trailing __x{hash} suffix over the structured signature
#1017: quantile is schema type:string — the QUOTED text is the contract. "0.990" slugs as q0_990 on BOTH sides verbatim (no numeric canonicalisation to 0.99); trailing zeros are shape identity.

#1017: dotless-exponent quantile text "95e-2" — the YAML-dialect ambiguity class that motivated string-only (bare, PyYAML 1.1 reads a string while yaml.v3 reads a float and Go re-canonicalises to 0.95). Quoted, both sides read the same text → q95e_2.
selector ordering must be deterministic: sorted by (key, op), regardless of YAML/map iteration order
grammar coverage: op omitted → default ">" → gt (must be identical Go/Python)
TRK-326: explicit for=5m enters the slug → distinct shape from the default-for (1m) twin above, so two tenants with different `for` compile to two distinct rules (no silent overwrite)
TRK-326: for=0s (fire-immediately) is a valid enum value and yields its own slug
forecast ratio mode (#741): capacity_metric set → headroom ratio; `h{horizon}` takes the window slot, capacity reuses the den_ slot; no window
forecast raw mode (#741): no capacity_metric → predict the gauge crossing an absolute threshold; no den_ part, no window
grammar coverage (#810): op "==" → eq (threshold-recipe-only; status/error-code-as-value match, e.g. MariaDB semi-sync errno 1236)

group_by (ADR-024 §Addendum): per-PVC disk-fill — `gb_persistentvolumeclaim` is appended LAST so a no-group_by slug stays byte-identical; the metric-side aggregation keeps the PVC dimension so a small full volume is not masked by a large empty one in a by(tenant) sum
#1008/F3 S1a: lossy selector value 'us-east-1' — pairs with S1b (both sanitise to s_region_us_east_1) but MUST yield DISTINCT recipe_ids via the __x suffix
#1008/F3 S1b: 'us_east_1' — MUST differ from S1a (collision resolved)
#1008/F3 S2a: NON-lossy separator aliasing — {region_x:1} flattens to s_region_x_1 with no lossy char; the structured-signature hash distinguishes it from S2b
#1008/F3 S2b: {region:x_1} flattens to the SAME s_region_x_1 — MUST differ from S2a (proves the hash is over the structured tuple, not the flat join)
